stories.title: FSU's 25-Game Win Streak Ends at Stanford

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Stanford ends FSU's 25-game winning streak.

The longest active streak in college softball. Gone.

Danley's double gave the Noles a brief lead — Stanford held on. 🥎

#Noles #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
FSU drops after the Stanford loss — but don't sleep on the Noles.

Isa Torres is still batting .610. Their staff is still elite.

First loss since February. The streak's over, the season is NOT. 🔥

#Noles #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 9:30 AM ET*

After 25 consecutive wins — the longest active streak in college softball — Florida State finally lost Friday night.

Stanford, ranked No. 23, held off a determined Seminole comeback on their home field in Palo Alto. FSU rallied and took a brief lead on a bases-clearing double by Ashtyn Danley, but the Cardinal answered to end the Noles' remarkable run.

For context: FSU had not lost a game since February. This was a streak that climbed into the top five in program history and was threatening even longer records.

The Seminoles are still one of the best teams in the country. Isa Torres is batting .610, their pitching staff is still elite, and they'll head into the rest of conference play with a real test now on their résumé. This might actually be good for them before the NCAA tournament.

But the streak is over — and that's genuinely stunning.

What do you think: Was this a wake-up call, or is FSU still a title contender?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with game photo*

Stanford just knocked off No. 9 FSU — snapping the longest active win streak in college softball at 25 games. The Noles fought back, but it wasn't enough.

stories.title: No. 1 Texas Tech Dominates Utah 10-2 in Road Game 1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Texas Tech. On the road. Four home runs. 12 hits.

UTAH NEVER HAD A CHANCE.

Red Raiders win 10-2 to open their six-game road trip. Now 39-2. 🥎

#WreckEm #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
NiJaree Canady pitched AND hit an RBI double at Utah.

Texas Tech 39-2 on the season.

Making the No. 1 national seed case look EASY. 🔥

#WreckEm #CollegeSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 12:00 PM ET*

No. 1 Texas Tech didn't waste any time making a statement on the road.

The Red Raiders traveled to Salt Lake City to open their six-game road trip and came away with a 10-2 demolition of Utah on Friday night. Four home runs. 12 hits. Total control. NiJaree Canady worked in the circle and added an RBI double herself.

Texas Tech is now 39-2 on the season with a 15-1 mark in Big 12 play. For perspective — their two losses are all that's keeping them from an undefeated season against one of the toughest schedules in the country.

Canady (15-2, 1.29 ERA heading into this road trip) is the defining player of the 2026 college softball season. She strikes out everyone, she handles the bat, and she makes her teammates better. Texas Tech winning 10-2 on the road like it's a home game is a testament to what she brings.

The Red Raiders are looking very much like a No. 1 national seed.

#WreckEm
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with action photo*

Four home runs. Canady in the circle. Texas Tech 10-2 in Utah. The Red Raiders are playing like no one can stop them.

stories.title: Red River Showdown — Oklahoma vs. Texas Series Opens at McCombs

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma. Texas. Austin. ESPN.

No. 2 Sooners vs. No. 4 Longhorns — the biggest series of the regular season.

OU led 3-0 late in Game 1. Game 2 tonight, 7 PM on ESPN. 👀

#BoomerSooner #HookEm #NCAASoftball

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
The SEC lead is on the line in Austin this weekend.

Oklahoma is 11-1 in conference. Texas is 9-3.

OU sweep = essentially over. Texas series win = we've got a race. 🔥

#BoomerSooner #HookEm #SECSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 1:30 PM ET*

The biggest series in college softball is underway in Austin.

No. 2 Oklahoma and No. 4 Texas — the reigning national champions — opened their three-game series Friday night at McCombs Field, and the drama started immediately. The Sooners led 3-0 heading into the late innings, putting early pressure on a Texas team that hasn't faced many deficits at home this year (the Longhorns are 17-1 at McCombs in 2026).

Game 2 is tonight at 7:00 PM ET on ESPN. Game 3 is Sunday at 1:00 PM ET on ESPN.

What makes this series special beyond the rivalry: The SEC lead is genuinely on the line. Oklahoma is 11-1 in conference play, Texas is 9-3. An OU sweep essentially locks up the SEC title for the Sooners. A Texas series win makes it a two-team race. Every pitch matters.

Then there's Kendall Wells — entering with 30 home runs and seven away from the all-time NCAA single-season record. Facing Texas pitching this weekend is the ultimate test.

This is college softball's marquee weekend. Don't miss Game 2 tonight.
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with stadium/rivalry photo*

Oklahoma vs. Texas is officially on. SEC lead, WCWS seeds, and history on the line — all in Austin this weekend.

stories.title: Kendall Wells at 30 Home Runs — Racing Toward All-Time NCAA Record

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells enters the Texas series with 30 home runs.

She needs SEVEN more to break the all-time NCAA single-season record.

Seven. As a freshman. In April. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET*

Kendall Wells is seven home runs away from rewriting the record books forever.

The Oklahoma freshman already has 30 home runs — an achievement that set the SEC single-season record and tied the NCAA freshman single-season record. She did it faster than anyone before her: No. 30 came in just her 40th game of the season.

Now she's chasing the all-time NCAA single-season mark of 37, set by Janie Reed Espinoza of Arizona back in 1995 — a record that has stood for 31 years.

Seven home runs might sound like a lot. But Wells is averaging better than a homer per every 1.5 games this season. She has the ability to get there.

The most exciting part? She gets to chase the record against Texas — the defending national champions — in the biggest series of the year. If Wells goes yard in Austin, the softball world stops.

Watch this weekend. You might be witnessing history.
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with Wells batting photo*

Seven home runs away from the all-time NCAA record. Kendall Wells enters the Texas series with history in her sights.

stories.title: Nebraska Blanks Wisconsin 5-0 — Big Ten Powerhouse Rolling

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nebraska blanks Wisconsin 5-0.

Their 10th shutout of the season. Now 31-6, 12-1 in the Big Ten.

Freshman Alexis Jensen in the circle. The Huskers are REAL. 👀

#Huskers #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 5:30 PM ET*

Nebraska might be the most underrated team in the country right now — and they keep proving it.

The Huskers shut out Wisconsin 5-0 on Friday night in Madison, recording their 10th shutout of the season and improving to 31-6 overall. In Big Ten play, they are an extraordinary 12-1. Freshman Alexis Jensen was excellent in the pitcher's circle.

Here's the thing about Nebraska that doesn't get enough national attention: 10 shutouts in 37 games is not a fluke. That's a pitching staff with real depth. And a 12-1 conference record against the Big Ten, which has become a legitimate softball power, is worth noting.

Nebraska isn't just running up numbers against weak opponents. They're dominating, and they're doing it consistently.

With regionals approaching in May, the Huskers are building the kind of profile that earns a top-16 national seed — and possibly a top-8.

Game 2 is today at noon CT in Madison. Keep watching.
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with Nebraska softball action photo*

Nebraska blanks Wisconsin 5-0 for their 10th shutout of the season. The Huskers are 12-1 in the Big Ten and quietly building a WCWS case.

stories.title: NFCA Week 9 Top 25 — The Rankings Picture Before a Big Weekend

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
NFCA Week 9 top five:

No. 1 Texas Tech
No. 2 Oklahoma
No. 3 Alabama
No. 4 Texas
No. 5 FSU

After this weekend? Looks completely different by Tuesday. 🥎

#NCAASoftball #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 3:30 PM ET*

The NFCA released their Week 9 top 25 on Tuesday — and the top of the rankings was exactly what you'd expect after a week full of dominant performances.

Texas Tech stayed at No. 1, followed by Oklahoma at No. 2, Alabama at No. 3, Texas at No. 4, and Florida State at No. 5. UCF returned to the rankings at No. 25, their first appearance in several weeks.

Here's the thing: Tuesday's Week 10 poll is going to look very different.

FSU just lost to Stanford, which almost certainly drops them from No. 5. Texas and Oklahoma are playing each other this weekend — one of them has to lose. Texas Tech is rolling at Utah but their ranking is safe.

Alabama, who's not playing a ranked opponent this weekend, could quietly move up just by staying undefeated.

The rankings are about to get interesting.
facebook.image_caption:
*Rankings graphic post*

NFCA Week 9: Texas Tech No. 1. Oklahoma No. 2. Alabama No. 3. Texas No. 4. FSU No. 5. That order is about to change in a big way.

stories.title: WCWS Watch — This Weekend's Results Will Shape the National Picture

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
This weekend's ranked matchups have WCWS seeds written all over them.

Red River series. Texas Tech at Utah. FSU at Stanford.

April games that feel like October. 🔥

#NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball #Softball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 8:00 PM ET*

The road to Oklahoma City just got more meaningful with every game this weekend.

Six ranked matchups are taking place across the country right now, and every result has direct implications for how the NCAA tournament field will look in May. The Red River series between Oklahoma and Texas is the headliner, but Texas Tech's six-game road trip, Nebraska's Big Ten push, and FSU's fight to recover at Stanford are all shaping the national picture simultaneously.

Texas is in the middle of the most brutal stretch of any team's schedule — 12 consecutive opponents ranked in the top 15 nationally. They're being tested in ways most programs never face.

Oklahoma needs to protect their SEC lead. Texas Tech needs to stay dominant on the road. Nebraska needs to close out the Big Ten.

It's only April — but April is when WCWS dreams get made or broken.

Which team do you have making the final eight in Oklahoma City this June?
facebook.image_caption:
*WCWS-themed post*

Every game this weekend is a playoff game in disguise. Red River. Big Ten titles. National seeds. It all matters right now.
